name: kimi-git-ask-downgraded-deny
doc: Kimi's hooks contract documents ONLY `permissionDecision:"deny"` — no "ask" value exists on the envelope (kimi.com/code/docs/en/kimi-code-cli/customization/hooks.html). respond.ts's kimi case therefore downgrades every `ask` prompt to an explicit deny (mirrors the pre-existing codex downgrade in the same function), prefixed so the honest limit is visible instead of silently failing open. A plain `git push` (GIT_ASK, an `ask` on Claude Code — see scenario 11) lands as a DENY on Kimi, never a silent allow.

steps.doc: `git push origin main` matches GIT_ASK (not GIT_BLOCKED) -> an `ask` prompt. On Kimi the `ask` downgrades to `deny`. Assert the decision is `deny`.
Same command, asserting the honest-limit prefix + harness name are present in the reason so the downgrade is visible.

stdout.contains: [downgraded from ask — Kimi Code has no interactive approval]
